subject: refactor(dashboard): migrate multi-select checkbox to DaisyUI checkbox
body:
Phase 10 (final) of the DaisyUI component-class migration. Audit
the FilterSidebar MultiSelect widget, document why the listbox
structure cannot move to DaisyUI `dropdown` + `menu`, and migrate
the inner checkbox to DaisyUI for visual consistency with the rest
of the filter sidebar.

Findings:
- Wrong semantic match: the MultiSelect widget implements the
  WAI-ARIA combobox/listbox pattern (role="listbox", role="option",
  aria-multiselectable="true"). DaisyUI's `menu` component implies
  role="menu" which is semantically wrong for multi-select filter
  values. DaisyUI v5 provides no first-class listbox component.
- DaisyUI's `dropdown` shows/hides via CSS :focus, which conflicts
  with the React state (`open` + `highlightIndex`) needed for the
  arrow-key navigation.
- No shadowing: `.filter-multi-select-*` is a unique prefix; DaisyUI
  does not define any class under that namespace, so the custom
  styles are safe.
- Theme cleanliness: the existing rules already use
  `var(--color-base-*)` tokens and `color-mix()` — no hardcoded
  theme values were found in this widget.

Changes:
- FilterSidebar.jsx: rationale block added to the MultiSelect
  component explaining the rejection reasoning so future audits
  do not re-open this question.
- FilterSidebar.jsx: the inner `<input type="checkbox">` gains
  `className="checkbox checkbox-xs checkbox-primary"` so it renders
  in the active theme's primary color instead of the browser
  default, matching the visual language of the rest of the
  DaisyUI-migrated filter sidebar.
- styles.css: the `.filter-multi-select-option input[type="checkbox"]`
  rule is slimmed down to only reset margin and flex-shrink — size,
  color, and cursor are now handled by DaisyUI's `checkbox` class.

Tags: ui, refactor, daisyui, migration, audit, accessibility
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-component-migration
Semver: patch
